title: pavonia viscidula
intro: Pavonia viscidula, also known as the sticky rose or Mexican rosemallow, is a species of flowering plant in the mallow family that is native to Mexico and Central America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of Pavonia viscidula include its ability to reduce swelling, promote tissue repair, and soothe digestive issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. In herbal preparations, the plant can be made into a salve, tincture, or infusion, and is often used topically to treat skin conditions and internally to support digestive health.
